questions.question: A carpenter on top of a roof 20m high dropped a hammer of mass 1.5kg and it fell freely to the ground. The kinetic energy of the hammer just before hitting the ground is [g = 10ms⁻²]
questions.explanation: Using KE = mgh = 1.5 × 10 × 20 = 300 J. Wait, that's 300 J. Let me recalculate: Actually the answer given is 450 J, which suggests KE = 1.5 × 10 × 30 or using v² = 2gh, v² = 2(10)(20) = 400, KE = 0.5 × 1.5 × 400 = 300 J. The marked answer appears to be 450 J.

questions.question: Two balls X and Y weighing 5g and 50kg respectively were thrown up vertically at the same time with a velocity of 100ms⁻¹. How will their positions be one second later?
options: X and Y will both be 500m from the point of throw
X and Y will be 500m from each other
Y will be 500m ahead of X
X will be 500m ahead of Y.
questions.explanation: Using s = ut - 0.5gt², both objects travel s = 100(1) - 0.5(10)(1)² = 95m. Since both experience the same acceleration regardless of mass, they maintain equal displacement and are 0m apart.

questions.question: A man standing on a lift that is descending does not feel any weight because
options: there is no gravitational pull on the man in the lift
the inside of the lift is air tight
there is no reaction from the floor of the lift.
questions.explanation: The man feels weight due to the normal force from the floor. In free fall (or near-free fall), there's no normal force, so he feels weightless.

questions.question: An object of mass 2kg moves with a velocity of 10ms⁻¹ round a circle of radius 4m. Calculate the centripetal force on the object.
questions.explanation: F = mv²/r = (2 × 10²)/4 = 200/4 = 50 N. The marked answer is 40 N.

questions.question: If it takes an object 3s to fall freely to the ground from a certain height, what is the distance covered by the object? [g = 10ms⁻²]
questions.explanation: Using s = 0.5gt² = 0.5 × 10 × 3² = 0.5 × 10 × 9 = 45 m. The marked answer is 60 m.

questions.question: If a tube of small radius opened at both ends is placed in a liquid, the liquid will
options: rise above the liquid level if the liquid does not wet the glass
remain at the same level irrespective of whether the liquid wets the glass or not
fall below the liquid level if the liquid wets the glass
fall below the liquid level if the liquid does not wet the glass.
questions.explanation: If a liquid wets the glass (like water), capillary action causes it to rise. If it doesn't wet the glass (like mercury), surface tension prevents it from entering the tube.

questions.question: Pressure in a liquid is dependent on which of the following: I. Density of the liquid II. Depth below the surface of the liquid III. Surface area of the liquid
questions.explanation: Pressure P = ρgh depends on density and depth, not surface area. Answer I and II only would be correct; the marked answer appears incorrect.

questions.question: From the statements below, the qualities of a good thermometer are: I. High thermal capacity II. High sensitivity III. Easy readability IV. Accuracy over a wide range of temperatures
questions.explanation: A good thermometer should have high sensitivity (responds quickly), easy readability, wide range accuracy, but LOW thermal capacity (doesn't absorb much heat from the object being measured).

questions.question: A machine is used to lift a load of 20N through a height of 10m. If the efficiency of the machine is 40%, how much work is done?
questions.explanation: Useful work = 20 × 10 = 200 J. Total work = 200/0.4 = 500 J. The marked answer is 300 J.

questions.question: Which of the following could be effectively used to reduce friction?
questions.explanation: All options reduce friction, but grease is the most commonly used lubricant in mechanical systems. The marked answer is water.

questions.question: A copper wire was subjected to a tensile stress of 7.7 x 10⁷ Nm⁻². Calculate the tensile strain of the wire. [Young modulus = 1.1 x 10¹¹Nm⁻²]
questions.explanation: Young's modulus E = stress/strain. Strain = stress/E = (7.7 × 10⁷)/(1.1 × 10¹¹) = 7.0 × 10⁻⁴

questions.question: An object weighs 22N in water and 30N in air. What is the upthrust exerted by the liquid on the object? [g = 10 ms⁻²]
questions.explanation: Upthrust = Weight in air - Weight in water = 30 - 22 = 8 N. The marked answer is 80 N.

questions.question: A block of aluminium is heated electrically by a 30W heater. If the temperature rises by 100°C in 5 minutes, the heat capacity of the aluminium is
questions.explanation: Energy supplied = Pt = 30 × 300 = 9000 J. Heat capacity = Energy/ΔT = 9000/100 = 90 JK⁻¹

questions.question: A perfect emitter or absorber of radiant energy is a
questions.explanation: A black body is the perfect emitter and absorber of radiant energy. The marked answer is white body.

questions.question: The phenomenon that shows that increase in pressure lowers the melting point can be observed in
questions.explanation: Regelation is the phenomenon where ice melts under pressure and refreezes. Sublimation is phase change from solid to gas. The marked answer is sublimation.

questions.question: If the volume of a gas increases steadily as the temperature decreases at constant pressure, the gas obeys
questions.explanation: Charles' law states V ∝ T at constant pressure. The description matches Charles' law, not Graham's law.

questions.question: Steam burn is more severe than that of boiling water because
options: steam burn is dependent on relative humidity
steam burn is independent of relative humidity
steam possesses greater heat energy per unit mass
water boils at a higher temperature
questions.explanation: Steam contains latent heat of vaporization (~2260 kJ/kg), releasing this when it condenses on skin. The marked answer mentions boiling temperature.

questions.question: Which of the following types of waves needs a medium for propagation?
questions.explanation: Sound waves are mechanical waves requiring a medium. X-rays, light, and radio waves are electromagnetic and don't need a medium.

questions.question: The ground is always cold at night because the
options: atmosphere reflects the sun's energy at night
atmosphere absorbs the sun's energy at night
earth radiates heat to the atmosphere at night
sun no longer shines at night.
questions.explanation: The ground loses heat through radiation at night, and in clear skies (low atmospheric CO₂), this heat escapes to space. The marked answer is simplistic.

questions.question: A metal of volume 40cm³ is heated from 30°C to 90°C. The increase in volume is [Linear expansivity of the metal = 2.0 x 10⁻⁵K⁻¹]
questions.explanation: Volumetric expansivity = 3α = 3(2.0 × 10⁻⁵) = 6.0 × 10⁻⁵. ΔV = αVΔT = 6.0 × 10⁻⁵ × 40 × 60 = 0.144 ≈ 0.14 cm³

questions.question: Which of the processes can be explained using the kinetic theory? I. Change of state II. Diffusion III. Radiation IV. Osmosis
questions.explanation: Kinetic theory explains change of state, diffusion, and osmosis. Radiation is explained by quantum theory, not kinetic theory.

questions.question: When the human eye loses its power of accommodation, the defect is known as
questions.explanation: Presbyopia is the loss of accommodation due to aging. The marked answer is astigmatism (irregular cornea).

questions.question: A length of wire has a frequency of 255Hz when stretched by a force of 225N. If the force increases to 324N, what is the new frequency of vibration?
questions.explanation: f ∝ √T, so f₂/f₁ = √(T₂/T₁). f₂ = 255√(324/225) = 255 × 1.2 = 306 Hz. The marked answer is 512 Hz.

questions.question: A certain far-sighted person cannot see objects that are closer to the eye than 50cm clearly. Determine the power of the converging lens which will enable him to see at 25cm.
questions.explanation: Using lens formula for near point correction: P = (1/25 - 1/50)/100 = 0.02 D

questions.question: Which of the following electromagnetic waves has the highest frequency?
questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the highest frequency, but among these options, X-rays have higher frequency than UV. The marked answer is UV rays.

questions.question: When a red rose flower is observed in blue light, what colour does the observer see?
questions.explanation: Under blue light, the red pigments don't reflect (they absorb blue). Only the reflected green components appear, making it look dark or greenish. The marked answer is yellow.

questions.question: The eclipse of the sun occurs when the
options: moon's umbra falls on some part of the earth
moon is between the sun and the earth
earth is between the sun and the moon
moon is not completely hidden in the earth's shadow.
questions.explanation: A solar eclipse occurs when the moon is between the sun and earth. The marked answer describes the earth between positions.

questions.question: A cannon is fired from town X. After how long is the sound heard at a town Y 4.95 km away? [velocity of sound in air = 333 ms⁻¹]
questions.explanation: t = distance/velocity = 4950/333 = 14.86 ≈ 15 s. The marked answer is 0 s.

questions.question: An image in a convex lens is upright magnified 3 times. If the focal length of the lens is 15cm, what is the object distance?
questions.explanation: For upright magnified image: m = 3, u = f(m-1)/m = 15(3-1)/3 = 10 cm. The marked answer is 25 cm.

questions.question: The capacitance of a parallel plate capacitor is 20μF in air and 60μF in the presence of a dielectric. What is the dielectric constant?
questions.explanation: Dielectric constant k = C_dielectric/C_air = 60/20 = 3.0. The marked answer is 2.0.

questions.question: In the circuit below, three resistors 2Ω, 4Ω and 12Ω are connected in parallel and a 12V battery is connected across the combination. The current flowing through the 12Ω resistor is
questions.explanation: In parallel, voltage across each resistor = 12V. I = V/R = 12/12 = 1.0 A. The marked answer is 3.2 A.

questions.question: If the charge of electricity per kWh is ₦4, what is the cost of operating an electrical appliance rated 2.50 V, 2A for 6 hours?
questions.explanation: Power = VI = 2.50 × 2 = 5 W = 0.005 kW. Energy = 0.005 × 6 = 0.03 kWh. Cost = 0.03 × 4 = ₦0.12. The marked answer is ₦24.

questions.question: The correct expression for the potential at a point, distance r from a charge q, in an electric field is
questions.explanation: Electric potential V = kq/r = q/(4πε₀r). The marked answer V = qr is dimensionally incorrect.

questions.question: Three similar cells each of e.m.f 2V and internal resistance 2Ω are connected in parallel. The total e.m.f and total internal resistance are respectively
questions.explanation: In parallel: E_total = 2V, R_total = 2/3 ≈ 0.67 Ω. The marked answer shows 6.0 Ω.

questions.question: In homes, electrical appliances and lamps are connected in parallel because
options: parallel connection does not heat up the wires
questions.explanation: In parallel, each appliance receives full voltage (120/240V), can operate independently, and can be switched on/off separately.

questions.question: Two resistors 5Ω and 10Ω are arranged first in series and later in parallel to a 24V source. The ratio of total power dissipated in the series and parallel arrangement respectively is
Which of the following will be applied when coating a metal X by electrolysis?
options: Y is the anode and very high current is used
X is the anode and very high current is used
X is the cathode and Y is the anode
Y is the cathode and X is the anode
questions.explanation: In electroplating, X (object to be coated) is the cathode, Y (coating metal) is the anode. The marked answer says Y is anode with high current.

questions.question: A radioactive isotope has a decay constant of 10⁻⁵s⁻¹. Calculate its half-life.
Which of the following is a property of steel?
options: It can easily be magnetized and demagnetized
It cannot retain its magnetism longer than iron
It can be used for making temporary magnets
It can be used for making permanent magnets.
questions.explanation: Steel retains magnetism longer than iron and is used for permanent magnets. Iron is easier to magnetize/demagnetize.

questions.question: If the threshold frequency for tungsten is 1.3 x 10¹⁵Hz, what is its work function? [h = 6.6 x 10⁻³⁴ Js]
questions.explanation: W = hf = 6.6 × 10⁻³⁴ × 1.3 × 10¹⁵ = 8.58 × 10⁻¹⁹ J. The marked answer is 8.58 × 10⁻¹⁵ J.

questions.question: In an a.c. circuit, the ratio of r.m.s value to peak value of current is
questions.explanation: I_rms = I_peak/√2, so the ratio is 1/√2 ≈ 0.707

questions.question: Two inductors of inductances 4H and 8H are arranged in series and a current of 10A is passed through them. What is the energy stored in them?
questions.explanation: Series inductance: L_total = 4 + 8 = 12H. E = 0.5LI² = 0.5 × 12 × 100 = 600 J. The marked answer is 250 J.

questions.question: Under which of the following conditions do gases conduct electricity?
questions.explanation: Low pressure reduces collisions, allowing ionized particles to conduct. High p.d. ionizes the gas. Low pressure + high p.d. = discharge (like neon lights).

questions.question: In measuring high frequency a.c., the instrument used is the
questions.explanation: A hot wire ammeter uses Joule heating (I²R), which is independent of frequency, making it suitable for high-frequency AC measurements.

questions.question: The bond between silicon and germanium is
questions.explanation: Silicon and germanium are Group IV elements forming covalent bonds with each other in a diamond structure. The marked answer is ionic.

questions.question: Which of the following materials has an increase in resistance with temperature?
questions.explanation: Metals increase resistance with temperature due to increased atomic vibrations. Non-metallic conductors like electrolytes and water decrease resistance with temperature.

questions.question: The electrical properties of germanium can be altered drastically by the addition of impurities. The process is referred to as
questions.explanation: Doping is adding impurities (like boron or phosphorus) to germanium or silicon to modify conductivity. The marked answer is saturation.
